summary: Bubble CPAP produces audible bubbling noises as gas passes through water, while high-flow nasal cannulae provide quiet delivery of warmed, humidified airflow. However, existing studies offer no measurable comparisons of their respective noise levels in preterm infants, so neither can be definitively stated as being more noisy—making such claims unsubstantiated by direct evidence.

However, existing studies offer no measurable comparisons of their respective noise levels in preterm infants, so neither can be definitively stated as being more noisy—making such claims unsubstantiated by direct evidence.. Answer choice: C
